title: rhus arborescens
intro: Rhus arborescens, also known as skunkbush or skunkbrush, is a type of shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns. The health benefits of Rhus arborescens include its potential to reduce pain, inflammation, and itching, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor burns. Rhus arborescens is often prepared into herbal teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
